held_out_queries: My abstract is 140 words. Is that too short for ASR, or do I need at least 150?
ASR rejected an earlier version of this paper last year. Can I revise and resubmit it to ASR?
Which edition of the ASA Style Guide should I format references to for ASR?

edits.summary: Correct the abstract rule (binding ceiling is 200 words or fewer, not a hard 150-200 band that wrongly rejects a 140-word abstract); add the 'ASR does not consider previously rejected papers' policy; pin the style edition (ASA Style Guide 7th ed., 2022, author-year); add the scope/fit desk-reject filter. $25 fee/waiver left 待核实.
edits.gate_reason: strict improvement 28 -> 100; corrected a behavior-changing wrong fact (over-narrow abstract band) plus two stranded stable facts; timeline/acceptance-rate/APC absent from source map, correctly not invented

result: asr-submission held-out 28 -> 100 (+72). Static scorecard ~94 unchanged. Baseline had a behavior-changing wrong fact (would wrongly tell a 140-word abstract to expand).
